title: cephalomanes crassum
intro: Cephalomanes crassum, also known as the thick-leaved cephala, is a rare species of fern native to tropical regions in Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cephalomanes crassum include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's fronds are often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
